benchnine.notes: Multiple corrupt plist structures (bare keys in arrays, missing values). Prebuild rewrites lib.plist with only glyphOrder.
prebuild:
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import os, glob
NL = chr(10)

for ufo in glob.glob('src/*.ufo'):
    lib_path = os.path.join(ufo, 'lib.plist')
    if not os.path.exists(lib_path):
        continue
    data = open(lib_path).read()
    
    glyph_order = []
    in_glyph_order = False
    in_array = False
    for line in data.split(NL):
        s = line.strip()
        if 'public.glyphOrder' in s:
            in_glyph_order = True
            continue
        if in_glyph_order and s == '<array>':
            in_array = True
            continue
        if in_glyph_order and in_array:
            if s == '</array>':
                in_glyph_order = False
                in_array = False
                continue
            if s.startswith('<string>'):
                val = s.replace('<string>', '').replace('</string>', '')
                glyph_order.append(val)
    
    lines = []
    lines.append('<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>')
    lines.append('<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple Computer//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">')
    lines.append('<plist version="1.0">')
    lines.append('<dict>')
    if glyph_order:
        lines.append('  <key>public.glyphOrder</key>')
        lines.append('  <array>')
        for g in glyph_order:
            lines.append(f'    <string>{g}</string>')
        lines.append('  </array>')
    lines.append('</dict>')
    lines.append('</plist>')
    open(lib_path, 'w').write(NL.join(lines) + NL)
    print(f'Rewrote {lib_path} ({len(glyph_order)} glyphOrder entries)')
PYEOF

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import os, glob

count = 0
for ufo in glob.glob('src/*.ufo'):
    glyphs_dir = os.path.join(ufo, 'glyphs')
    if not os.path.isdir(glyphs_dir):
        continue
    for fname in os.listdir(glyphs_dir):
        if not fname.endswith('.glif'):
            continue
        fpath = os.path.join(glyphs_dir, fname)
        lines = open(fpath).readlines()
        new_lines = [l for l in lines if 'com.typemytype.robofont.layerData' not in l]
        if len(new_lines) != len(lines):
            open(fpath, 'w').writelines(new_lines)
            count += 1
print(f'Fixed {count} glyphs with empty layerData key')
PYEOF

bigshotone.notes: Prebuild: comment out references to undefined glyph classes in features.fea
prebuild:
python3 << 'EOF'
import os, re

fea = 'src/BigshotOne.ufo/features.fea'
if os.path.exists(fea):
    data = open(fea).read()
    
    # Find all glyph class definitions (lines with @class = [...])
    defined_classes = set()
    for m in re.finditer(r'@(\w+)\s*=\s*\[', data):
        defined_classes.add(m.group(1))
    
    # Find all glyph class references
    referenced = set()
    for m in re.finditer(r'@(\w+)', data):
        referenced.add(m.group(1))
    
    undefined = referenced - defined_classes
    if undefined:
        print(f'Undefined classes: {undefined}')
        lines = data.split('\n')
        fixed = []
        for line in lines:
            should_comment = False
            for cls in undefined:
                if '@' + cls in line and not line.strip().startswith('#'):
                    should_comment = True
                    break
            if should_comment:
                fixed.append('# ' + line + '  # DISABLED: undefined glyph class')
            else:
                fixed.append(line)
        open(fea, 'w').write('\n'.join(fixed))
        print(f'Commented out references to undefined classes')
EOF

from glyphsLib import GSFont
font = GSFont('sources/BioRhyme.glyphs')

# Activate expanded instances (wdth=125), deactivate normal (wdth=100)
for inst in font.instances:
    if inst.axes[1] == 125:
        inst.active = True
        print(f'Activated expanded: {inst.name}')
    else:
        inst.active = False
        print(f'Deactivated normal: {inst.name}')

# Set family name to BioRhyme Expanded
font.familyName = 'BioRhyme Expanded'
print(f'Set familyName to: {font.familyName}')

font.save('sources/BioRhyme.glyphs')
print('Saved')
PYEOF

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import os, plistlib, glob

for ufo in glob.glob('sources/2-build/Bungee_Color/*.ufo'):
    fontinfo = os.path.join(ufo, 'fontinfo.plist')
    if os.path.exists(fontinfo):
        with open(fontinfo, 'rb') as f:
            info = plistlib.load(f)
        for key in ['familyName', 'openTypeNamePreferredFamilyName']:
            if key in info and '_' in str(info[key]):
                info[key] = info[key].replace('_', '')
        with open(fontinfo, 'wb') as f:
            plistlib.dump(info, f)
    
    # Only rename the UFO basename, keep parent dir
    parent = os.path.dirname(ufo)
    basename = os.path.basename(ufo)
    new_basename = basename.replace('Bungee_Color', 'BungeeColor')
    if new_basename != basename:
        new_ufo = os.path.join(parent, new_basename)
        os.rename(ufo, new_ufo)
        print(f'Renamed {basename} -> {new_basename}')
PYEOF

cambay.notes: Prebuild: comment out all missing Devanagari glyph refs in features.fea
prebuild:
python3 << 'EOF'
import os, plistlib, re

for root, dirs, files in os.walk('.'):
    if root.endswith('.ufo') and 'features.fea' in files:
        fea_path = os.path.join(root, 'features.fea')
        contents_path = os.path.join(root, 'glyphs', 'contents.plist')
        
        if not os.path.exists(contents_path):
            continue
        
        with open(contents_path, 'rb') as f:
            contents = plistlib.load(f)
        glyph_names = set(contents.keys())
        
        data = open(fea_path).read()
        lines = data.split('\n')
        fixed = []
        modified = False
        
        for line in lines:
            stripped = line.strip()
            if stripped.startswith('#') or not stripped:
                fixed.append(line)
                continue
            
            # Check if this line references glyphs (sub X by Y patterns)
            # Extract all potential glyph names from the line
            has_missing = False
            # Match "word-word" patterns that could be glyph names
            for m in re.finditer(r'\b([a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+(?:-[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+)*)\b', stripped):
                name = m.group(1)
                # Skip OpenType keywords
                if name in ('by', 'from', 'sub', 'pos', 'lookup', 'feature', 'table',
                           'include', 'script', 'language', 'subtable', 'name',
                           'parameters', 'HorizAxis', 'VertAxis', 'position'):
                    continue
                if '-deva' in name or '_deva' in name or '-hindi' in name:
                    if name not in glyph_names:
                        has_missing = True
                        break
            
            if has_missing:
                fixed.append('# ' + line + '  # DISABLED: missing glyph')
                modified = True
            else:
                fixed.append(line)
        
        if modified:
            open(fea_path, 'w').write('\n'.join(fixed))
            print(f'Fixed {fea_path}')
EOF

import os, plistlib

# Rename Regular UFO

gayathri.notes: Prebuild: add instances to designspace + symlink features dir for instance UFOs
prebuild:
python3 << 'EOF'
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
import os

ds_path = 'sources/Gayathri.designspace'
tree = ET.parse(ds_path)
root = tree.getroot()

if root.find('instances') is None:
    instances = ET.SubElement(root, 'instances')
    for name, weight in [('Gayathri-Thin', 100), ('Gayathri-Regular', 400), ('Gayathri-Bold', 700)]:
        inst = ET.SubElement(instances, 'instance')
        inst.set('familyname', 'Gayathri')
        inst.set('stylename', name.split('-')[1])
        inst.set('name', name)
        inst.set('filename', f'instance_ufos/{name}.ufo')
        loc = ET.SubElement(inst, 'location')
        dim = ET.SubElement(loc, 'dimension')
        dim.set('name', 'weight')
        dim.set('xvalue', str(weight))
    ET.indent(tree, space='\t')
    tree.write(ds_path, xml_declaration=True, encoding='utf-8')
    print('Added 3 instances to designspace')

os.makedirs('sources/instance_ufos', exist_ok=True)
for target, src in [('sources/instance_ufos/features', '../features'), ('features', 'sources/features')]:
    if not os.path.exists(target):
        os.symlink(src, target)
        print(f'Created symlink {target}')
EOF

kalam.notes: Prebuild: fix instances, axis mapping, string kerning values, and string descender in Kalam.glyphs
prebuild:
python3 << 'PYEOF2'
from glyphsLib import GSFont, GSInstance, GSCustomParameter

font = GSFont('masters/Kalam.glyphs')

# Remove degenerate wdth axis (both masters have same width value)
wdth_idx = None
for i, axis in enumerate(font.axes):
    if axis.axisTag == 'wdth':
        wdth_idx = i
        break
if wdth_idx is not None:
    del font.axes[wdth_idx]
    for master in font.masters:
        master.axes = list(master.axes[:wdth_idx]) + list(master.axes[wdth_idx+1:])
    print('Removed degenerate wdth axis')

# Fix master numeric values that are stored as strings
for m in font.masters:
    if isinstance(m.descender, str):
        m.descender = int(m.descender)
        print(f'Fixed descender for master {m.name}')

# Fix existing instance (name is integer 50, should be string)
font.instances[0].name = "Regular"
font.instances[0].axes = [50]

# Add Light instance
light = GSInstance()
light.name = "Light"
light.axes = [0]
font.instances.insert(0, light)

# Add Bold instance
bold = GSInstance()
bold.name = "Bold"
bold.axes = [100]
font.instances.append(bold)

# Add weightClass to each instance
for inst in font.instances:
    if inst.name == "Light":
        inst.customParameters.append(GSCustomParameter('weightClass', 300))
    elif inst.name == "Regular":
        inst.customParameters.append(GSCustomParameter('weightClass', 400))
    elif inst.name == "Bold":
        inst.customParameters.append(GSCustomParameter('weightClass', 700))

# Add axis mapping: user -> design coordinates
font.customParameters.append(GSCustomParameter('Axis Mappings', {
    'wght': {'300': 0, '400': 50, '700': 100}
}))

# Fix kerning values: convert string values to integers
fixed = 0
for master_id in font.kerning:
    for left in font.kerning[master_id]:
        for right in font.kerning[master_id][left]:
            val = font.kerning[master_id][left][right]
            if isinstance(val, str):
                font.kerning[master_id][left][right] = int(val)
                fixed += 1
print(f'Fixed {fixed} kerning values from string to int')

font.save('masters/Kalam.glyphs')
print('Patched Kalam.glyphs: fixed instances, axis mapping, kerning, and descender')
PYEOF2

khula.notes: SP3 source only - no modern build pipeline support

lancelot.notes: VFB source only - gftools cannot build .vfb files (requires FontLab conversion)
lancelot.failure_message: SFD/VFB source format not fully supported

notosans.notes: Massive build (599 steps) with includeSubsets. Likely timeout.

notosansdeseret.notes: Cohort venv build failed. Needs different tool versions.

notosansduployan.notes: Procedurally generated font — sources are Python scripts, not .glyphs/.ufo. Fonts shipped from release archive (archive_url). No standard build pipeline possible.
notosansduployan.failure_message: No .glyphs/.ufo sources in repo — font is procedurally generated

notosanskhojki.notes: Single master with degenerate wght+wdth axes. Prebuild removes axes.
prebuild:
python3 << 'PYEOF'
from glyphsLib import GSFont

font = GSFont('sources/NotoSansKhojki.glyphs')
# Remove all axes (single master, no variation)
font.axes = []
for m in font.masters:
    m.axes = []
for inst in font.instances:
    inst.axes = []
font.save('sources/NotoSansKhojki.glyphs')
print('Removed degenerate axes from single-master source')
PYEOF

notosanstifinagh.notes: Single master with degenerate wght/wdth axes and 12 subset instances. Only need main NotoSansTifinagh. Prebuild strips axes and extra instances.
prebuild:
cd sources && python3 << 'PYEOF'
from glyphsLib import GSFont
f = GSFont('NotoSansTifinagh.glyphs')
f.axes = []
for m in f.masters:
    m.axes = []
main_inst = f.instances[0]
main_inst.axes = []
f.instances = [main_inst]
f.save('NotoSansTifinagh.glyphs')
print('Kept main instance [0], removed axes and 11 subset instances')
PYEOF

playfairdisplay.notes: Prebuild fixes: (1) remove duplicate smart component layers with timestamp suffixes, (2) add missing smart component axis mappings to partSelection blocks, (3) fix wrong pole values causing duplicate locations. After prebuild, build still fails with glyph compatibility errors: ~50 glyphs have differing contour/component counts across optical size masters (bhook, bstroke, q, u, many Cyrillic glyphs). Both Roman and Italic affected. Source at commit 80a3341 requires Glyphs 3 smart component resolution that fontmake/glyphsLib cannot reproduce.

playfairdisplaysc.notes: Same upstream source as playfairdisplay (clauseggers/Playfair). Same build failures apply. See playfairdisplay notes.

sawarabimincho.notes: SFD source requires FontForge for conversion. babelfont cannot read this format.

susemono.notes: Roman VF builds successfully (metadata-only diff). Italic excluded due to incompatible masters.
susemono.failure_message: Italic excluded: incompatible designspace. Roman builds with metadata-only differences.

tiktoksans.notes: Build succeeds but produces [opsz,wdth,wght]. Shipped font has [opsz,slnt,wdth,wght]. slnt axis requires upstream preprocessing script (scripts/build/glyphsToUFO.py) to generate oblique masters.
tiktoksans.failure_message: Source requires custom build script (glyphsToUFO.py) to generate slanted masters before gftools-builder

tiny5.notes: SFD source requires FontForge for conversion. babelfont cannot read this format.

vollkorn.notes: Build fails with current toolchain. Needs matched tool versions.

zcoolkuaile.notes: Cohort 0.9.98 test: custom isolation did not achieve byte-identical (compiler-version)
